recipes.title: Couscous
recipes.description: Algerian Friday couscous of fluffy steamed semolina topped with lamb, chickpeas, and a saffron-tinted vegetable broth.
steps: Soak chickpeas overnight, then drain.
Brown cubed lamb in olive oil with chopped onions; stir in tomato paste, ras el hanout, and saffron.
Add chickpeas, salt, and 2 liters water; simmer 1 hour.
Moisten couscous with cold salted water, rake with fingers, and steam over the broth in a couscoussier for 20 minutes.
Tip into a tray, drizzle with oil, and rake again to separate; steam another 20 minutes.
Add chunks of carrot, turnip, and potato to the broth; simmer 20 minutes.
Add courgette and cook 10 more minutes until vegetables are just tender.
Steam couscous a final 15 minutes; mound on a platter, top with meat and vegetables, and ladle the broth over.

recipes.title: Chorba Frik
recipes.description: Algerian Ramadan soup of cracked freekeh wheat, lamb, tomato, and chickpeas, perfumed with cinnamon and mint.
steps: Soak chickpeas overnight, then drain.
Brown cubed lamb in olive oil with chopped onion and celery.
Stir in tomato paste, cinnamon, and ras el hanout; cook 1 minute.
Add chopped tomatoes, soaked chickpeas, salt, and 2 liters water; simmer 1 hour.
Rinse freekeh and add to the pot; cook 30 minutes until tender.
Adjust salt and add hot water if too thick.
Stir in chopped mint and serve hot with lemon for iftar.

recipes.title: Rechta
recipes.description: Algerian hand-cut fresh egg noodles steamed and tossed with chicken, chickpeas, and turnip in a cinnamon broth.
steps: Soak chickpeas overnight, then drain.
Knead flour, eggs, salt, and a little water into a stiff dough; rest 30 minutes.
Roll the dough as thinly as possible and cut into long thin noodles; toss with flour to prevent sticking.
Brown chicken with chopped onion in olive oil, then add cinnamon and white pepper.
Add chickpeas, salt, and 1.5 liters water; simmer 45 minutes.
Add cubed turnip and cook 20 minutes more.
Steam the rechta noodles over the broth in a couscoussier for 15 minutes, then toss with butter.
Mound the noodles on a platter, top with chicken, chickpeas, and turnip, and ladle over the cinnamon broth.

recipes.title: Mhajeb
recipes.description: Algerian semolina flatbread folded around tomato, onion, and pepper filling, then pan-cooked until crisp and flaky.
steps: Mix semolina, flour, salt, and water into a soft dough; knead 10 minutes and rest in oil for 30 minutes.
Sauté chopped onion in olive oil until soft; add chopped pepper, cumin, paprika, and harissa.
Stir in chopped tomatoes and tomato paste; simmer 15 minutes until thick and dry. Cool completely.
Divide dough into 8 balls and oil generously; let rest another 15 minutes.
Stretch each ball paper-thin on a well-oiled surface.
Spoon a portion of cooled filling in the center, fold all four sides over to form a square parcel.
Cook on a hot dry skillet for 3 minutes per side until crisp and golden.
Serve hot with mint tea.

recipes.title: Chakhchoukha
recipes.description: Algerian Aurès dish of torn flat bread tossed in a spiced lamb and chickpea tomato stew until soft and saturated.
steps: Soak chickpeas overnight, then drain.
Knead flour, salt, and water into a soft dough; divide into balls and stretch each into a thin disc.
Cook the discs on a dry skillet for 2 minutes per side until just set; stack and cool.
Brown cubed lamb in olive oil with chopped onions; stir in tomato paste, ras el hanout, and harissa.
Add chopped tomatoes, chickpeas, salt, and 1.5 liters water; simmer 1.5 hours until very tender.
Tear the bread discs into rough small pieces.
Pile the bread on a platter, ladle the stew over to soak, and toss gently to coat.
Top with extra meat and chickpeas and a drizzle of ghee.

recipes.title: Bourek
recipes.description: Algerian crispy fried cigar pastries of thin dough wrapped around minced meat, onion, and parsley, sealed with egg.
steps: Brown minced beef with chopped onion, ras el hanout, cinnamon, salt, and pepper until dry.
Stir in chopped parsley off the heat; cool completely.
Lay a brik sheet on the work surface and place a tablespoon of filling in a strip near one edge.
Sprinkle a little cheese, fold sides in, and roll into a tight cigar.
Brush the edge with beaten egg to seal.
Heat oil to 180C and fry borek in batches for 3 minutes until deep golden.
Drain on paper and serve hot with lemon wedges.

recipes.title: Loubia
recipes.description: Algerian white bean stew slow-simmered with tomato, garlic, paprika, and cumin, finished with olive oil and parsley.
steps: Soak beans overnight, then drain.
Sauté chopped onion in olive oil until soft, then add minced garlic.
Stir in tomato paste, paprika, cumin, and harissa; cook 1 minute.
Add chopped tomatoes, soaked beans, salt, and 1.5 liters water.
Simmer covered for 1.5 hours until beans are creamy and the broth has thickened.
Reduce uncovered for 10 minutes if too thin.
Drizzle with extra olive oil and shower with chopped parsley before serving with crusty bread.

recipes.title: Makroudh
recipes.description: Algerian semolina diamonds stuffed with date paste, fried, and dipped in honey scented with orange blossom.
steps: Rub semolina with melted ghee and salt until sandy; rest 1 hour.
Add warm water and 1 tablespoon orange blossom water; knead just until it holds.
Mash dates with cinnamon and 1 tablespoon orange blossom water into a smooth paste.
Roll dough into long ropes; lay a rope of date paste down the center, fold over, and seal.
Cut on the diagonal into small diamonds and press a pattern with a fork.
Fry in 170C oil for 3 minutes until pale gold.
Warm honey with the remaining orange blossom water and dip the hot pastries to coat.
Drain on a rack and let the syrup set before serving with mint tea.

recipes.title: Shakshouka
recipes.description: Algerian skillet of peppers and tomatoes simmered with garlic and harissa, finished with eggs poached on top.
steps: Sauté chopped onion and peppers in olive oil for 10 minutes until softened and beginning to char.
Add minced garlic, cumin, paprika, and harissa; cook 30 seconds.
Stir in chopped tomatoes and salt and simmer 15 minutes until thickened.
Make 6 wells in the sauce and crack an egg into each.
Cover and cook on low heat for 6 minutes until whites are set but yolks remain runny.
Scatter chopped parsley and serve straight from the pan with crusty bread.
